mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment (UNRATE below 4%) signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4% but SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of months, or Rank IC across all periods is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise of the federal funds rate above 3% signals an inflection point where monetary tightening begins to compress equity valuations and slow economic activity, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 3%, the average 20-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over expanding windows.

mechanism_narrative: When both CPI inflation is elevated (CPIAUCSL > 255) and unemployment is very low (UNRATE < 3.8), the economy is overheating; this combination forces aggressive Fed action and leads to negative forward returns as expectations of tightening rise.
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields average 20-day returns that are not below the unconditional average by at least 2 standard errors, or if the interaction term in a regression is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL < 260), a rise in housing starts above 1200 units signals a strengthening housing sector that drives consumer confidence and spending, generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If during low CPI periods (CPIAUCSL < 260) the signal HOUST>1200 produces a 60-day forward return rank IC below 0.05 or average return not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below the 108 level signals weakening manufacturing activity that leads to reduced corporate earnings and negative short-term market returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after INDPRO falls below 108 is not materially negative (mean < -0.5%) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls are above 151,000 (strong employment) and retail sales exceed $500 billion (strong consumption), the economy is running hot; this combination foreshadows tightening by the Fed, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence of these conditions does not lead to average 20-day returns that are less than the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point in a significant proportion of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of strong industrial growth (INDPRO > 105), increases in the federal funds rate are perceived as normalization rather than tightening, boosting bank profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in periods with INDPRO > 105, the 60-day forward return after a FEDFUNDS rise above 2.0 is not positive on average (one-sided test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The quarterly real GDP level above 18,900 billion chained dollars indicates an economy operating well above potential; historically this foreshadows mean reversion and negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter as growth slows.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day return following a GDP reading above 18,900 is not significantly negative (p < 0.10) over the last 10 years.
